executiveSummary:
KCE_Demo_Rebuild is a single-page item-management application backed by a layered FastAPI service over PostgreSQL. Users create, browse, toggle completion on, and delete items through a React front end that talks to a small REST API. The backend mirrors those operations as System-persona flows and adds an immutable audit trail: PostgreSQL triggers record every INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE on the items table into an audit_logs table, exposed through a read-only audit endpoint. A health probe rounds out the surface.

The functional graph covers two personas (User, System), four outcomes, ten scenarios, and nine REST APIs. The design is deliberately thin: no authentication guards, query-parameter inputs, and trigger-driven auditing rather than application-level logging.
keyBusinessObjectives: Let users manage a list of items end to end (create, read, update status, delete)
Guarantee a tamper-evident audit trail for all item mutations via database triggers
Expose item operations through a small, predictable REST contract
Provide a health probe for uptime monitoring
Keep the stack thin and demonstrable for rebuild/reference purposes

keyStakeholders.interest: Fast, direct CRUD over items with immediate list refresh and visible completion state
Correct request handling, existence validation, persistence, and automatic audit capture

keyCapabilities: Item creation with required name and description

User.description: The end user of the single-page app who manages items and monitors backend status and the item list.

System.description: The backend service that handles REST requests, validates and persists item data, and records audit entries via PostgreSQL triggers.

06dd9b83-3e48-46e8-9aa1-df7098e3acb1.businessValue: Gives users a complete, self-service item workflow on one page, removing any dependency on backend operators for routine list management.
06dd9b83-3e48-46e8-9aa1-df7098e3acb1.mermaidDiagram:
graph TD
  U[User] --> ADD[Add item]
  U --> BROWSE[Browse and monitor list]
  U --> TOGGLE[Toggle completion]
  U --> DEL[Remove item]
  ADD --> API[(Items REST API)]
  TOGGLE --> API
  DEL --> API
  BROWSE --> API

ce3c7fe7-d223-4722-817f-0848f36e4b9a.businessValue: Centralizes item CRUD with existence validation and automatic audit capture, so data integrity and traceability hold without extra application code.
ce3c7fe7-d223-4722-817f-0848f36e4b9a.mermaidDiagram:
graph TD
  REQ[REST request] --> CREATE[Create item]
  REQ --> LIST[List items]
  REQ --> UPD[Update status]
  REQ --> DELETE[Delete item]
  CREATE --> ITEMS[(items table)]
  UPD --> ITEMS
  DELETE --> ITEMS
  ITEMS -->|trigger| AUDIT[(audit_logs)]

70432abd-1ad4-4196-80d3-6aafc0c62550.businessValue: Surfaces the immutable audit trail so changes to tracked tables can be reviewed without direct database access.
70432abd-1ad4-4196-80d3-6aafc0c62550.mermaidDiagram:
graph TD
  REQ[GET /api/audit] --> SVC[AuditService]
  SVC --> REPO[AuditRepository]
  REPO --> LOGS[(audit_logs)]
  LOGS --> RESP[List AuditLogResponse]

e6449cb3-57d5-4e9b-8b3d-90a44a25b527.businessValue: Provides a dependency-free liveness signal for uptime monitoring and deployment checks.
e6449cb3-57d5-4e9b-8b3d-90a44a25b527.mermaidDiagram:
graph TD
  PROBE[GET /] --> H[read_root handler]
  H --> OK[200 status ok]
